# ValueTempo

> The AVS Rubric scores AI product buyability — how well buyers and AI agents can independently evaluate, budget for, and justify a purchase across 8 trust dimensions.

ValueTempo measures the public trust infrastructure of AI-native B2B products. Each scan analyzes pricing pages, docs, trust centers, and policy surfaces using a deterministic rubric and returns a 0–16 score with evidence-based rationale.

## Pages

- [Home](/): Run a free AVS Rubric scan on any AI product URL.
- [Methodology](/methodology): How the AVS Rubric scoring engine works.
- [FAQ](/faq): Answers for Product, Growth, CFO, and RevOps teams.
- [Privacy](/privacy): Data handling and CCPA/CPRA disclosures.

## Resources

- [Blog](/resources/blog): Essays on trust infrastructure and revenue quality.
- [The Verification Loop](/resources/blog/verification-loop): Case-study post on iterating an AVS score.
- [Stable Score, Unstable Evidence](/resources/blog/stable-score-unstable-evidence): Engine tuning notes.
- [Clay's Three-Layer Pricing](/resources/blog/clay-pricing-three-layers): Pricing structure breakdown.
- [Trust as the Growth Constraint](/resources/blog/trust-growth-constraint): Why trust is the bottleneck.
- [Vibecoding the AI Startup Tool](/resources/blog/vibecoding-ai-startup-tool): Build-in-public notes.
- [Case Studies](/resources/case-studies): Real-world AVS Rubric analyses.
- [Clay Case Study](/resources/case-studies/clay): Pricing transparency teardown.
- [ElevenLabs Case Study](/resources/case-studies/elevenlabs): Voice AI buyability analysis.
